generatedBy: /audit skill v2 · Session 76 founder-requested infrastructure rubric + public app/game release lens
summary: Premise-verified Session 76 infrastructure audit: eliminate arithmetic and parser-based false observability, make project/release metadata fail closed across generated surfaces, enforce complete footer navigation, and bind operator documentation to the immutable staging-to-promotion contract without fabricating live launch evidence.

sample.method: main-agent live verification plus one read-only app-release-gate lane; no candidate accepted from prose alone
sample.scanned: startup meter and SIL forecast, project/status/manifest truth, doctor probes, release evidence, footer leaves and manifests, deploy/promote workflows, operator runbook, CI, TODO markers, and control-plane release gate
sample.baseline: tree began clean and one commit ahead; doctor 7/7 blockingFailing 0; 814/814 recorded tests; local release evidence blocked; canonical control-plane release checker falsely ready

evidenceBoundary: Local tests cannot satisfy real staging, parity, distinct-human Alpha, Brevo delivery, Obelisk relying-party, live header/Core Web Vitals/theme, revenue, or founder-approval gates.
Registry and generated manifest metadata disagree with local PROJECT_STATUS; sibling source files will not be edited, and central corrections must travel as Ark cargo.
The current local release evidence is correctly blocked; the defect is split-brain metadata and a canonical checker that accepts incomplete gate models.
No package installation or license change is required. AGPL upstream obligations and VaultSpark-original rights posture remain unchanged.

items.title: Derive brief context usage from tokens and limit instead of an ambiguous percentage field
items.why: render-startup-brief treats context-meter pctUsed=0.8 as a fraction and multiplies it to 80 even though the producer documents pctUsed as percentage points; the same brief prints 8,331/1,000,000 beside the false 80% claim.
items.coreLoopStep: operator input → trustworthy continuation fantasy → saturated-work reward → clean closeout progression
items.recipe: Create one pure usage derivation contract from usedTokens/limit, import it into the renderer, cover percentage-point and fraction-shaped fixtures, regenerate the brief, and assert the rendered arithmetic cannot drift.

L2.recipe: Add a pure shared helper, unit tests, and live brief verification.

L3.recipe: Add semantic brief validation that rejects token/percent contradictions.

executionLog.evidence: Pure token/limit derivation, renderer integration, semantic brief sentinel, adversarial unit tests, and live 3% brief arithmetic.

items.title: Make generated project identity disagree loudly and remove the false-ready release posture
items.why: PROJECT_STATUS says game/public-unlaunched/alpha while STUDIO_MANIFEST says game/internal/concept, privateByDefault=true, brandingRequired=false, and publicRepoSanitized=false; the canonical release checker consumes that shape and returns ready despite seven explicit blocked gates.
items.coreLoopStep: release evidence input → trustworthy command fantasy → honest gate reward → FORGE-to-SPARKED progression
items.recipe: Extend project-truth evaluation to compare status and manifest identity/public metadata, repair the local manifest to current public-safe truth, add adversarial fixtures, rerun doctor and the canonical release check, and ship signed Ark correction cargo for the registry/checker source rather than editing the sibling tree.

L2.recipe: Add fail-closed cross-surface truth checks, fixtures, local repair, and Ark evidence.

L3.recipe: Bind a canonical identity fingerprint into release evidence and control-plane admission.

executionLog.evidence: Cross-surface truth evaluator, repaired public-unlaunched manifest, tamper-sensitive fingerprint, release-lineage binding, focused doctor tests, and Ark correction queued for the canonical source.

items.title: Parse every current SIL header/table shape and refuse zero-evidence forecasts
items.why: sil-forecaster recognizes only one legacy heading and indexed table rows; Session 75 uses a pipe Total heading and non-indexed category rows, so the forecast aggregates zero categories into 0/1000 while claiming all categories are stable.
items.coreLoopStep: SIL evidence input → credible next-session fantasy → prioritized-work reward → capability progression
items.recipe: Parse Sprint and pipe-Total headings, support indexed and non-indexed category tables, sort by actual session recency, return null when no categories exist, and test current plus legacy shapes before regenerating the brief.

L1.recipe: Patch the heading and row regexes.

L2.recipe: Use structural row parsing, recency sorting, no-evidence refusal, and regression fixtures.

L3.recipe: Schema-version SIL entries and derive forecasts from structured JSON.

executionLog.evidence: Current and legacy SIL structural parsing, recency ordering, null-on-no-evidence behavior, regression tests, and live 983/1000 brief forecast.

items.title: Make the operator runbook executable against the immutable staging and promotion workflows
items.why: DEPLOY_RUNTIME_RUNBOOK tells operators to deploy production from Deploy and rollback with image_tag, while deploy.yml is staging-only and promote.yml requires image_digest plus staging_evidence_digest and defaults to dry_run.
items.coreLoopStep: verified staging digest input → safe-promotion fantasy → rollback-confidence reward → release progression
items.recipe: Rewrite deploy/promotion/rollback steps around exact digests, matching staging evidence, dry-run-first behavior, commit verification, and rollback drill receipts; extend check-deploy-contract so obsolete input names or workflow-incompatible instructions fail CI/doctor.

L1.recipe: Correct the obsolete workflow input names.

L2.recipe: Replace the full operator sequence and make documentation tokens executable in the contract checker.

L3.recipe: Generate an attested rollback receipt and rehearse it on staging.

executionLog.evidence: Digest-addressed staging/promotion/rollback runbook and executable 25-check workflow/document contract.

items.title: Make every public leaf footer a complete route map and reject vacuous manifests
items.why: leaf headers expose Play/About/How to play/Contact while footers expose only legal links; the local checker searches whole HTML, and the canonical checker accepts the incompatible manifest as a zero-header/zero-footer pass.
items.coreLoopStep: visitor navigation input → discoverable game fantasy → trust/contact reward → alpha-conversion progression
items.recipe: Adopt the canonical headerLinks/footerLinks/footerOnly/legalPages schema, require non-empty sets, extract nav and footer scopes separately, enforce footer ⊇ header ∪ footer-only on every leaf, update all ten leaves, and add a header-only false-pass fixture.

L1.recipe: Add missing destinations to all footers.

L2.recipe: Ship canonical schema, scoped parser, exhaustive leaves, and adversarial tests.

L3.recipe: Generate the route graph from one source and visually verify every theme on staging.

executionLog.evidence: Schema v2 non-vacuous route contract, scoped nav/footer parser, ten complete public leaves, canonical checker pass, and false-pass fixtures.

premiseRejections.evidence: generateGameIdForWorker is bounded to 1,000 attempts and validates config.workerIndex for every candidate; current worker counts make exhaustion negligible, and create admission already rejects collision/capacity. No live failure evidence exists.
schema explicitly defers the view until DATABASE_URL is confirmed in production; staging and database evidence are absent, so migration now would be speculative and cannot clear a gate.
the TODO surrounds one authenticated getUserMe call during client startup, not a repeated hot path; no request-volume or latency evidence supports infrastructure priority.
staging, parity, Brevo, Obelisk, live theme/header/Core Web Vitals, distinct-human Alpha, revenue, and founder approval are explicitly non-substitutable; fabricating them would violate CANON-031.
